path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 14 - Game & Watch (Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2001)\01. Flat Zone.mp3
description: Imagine you're making music for the Game & Watch stage. Honestly, kudos to Hirokazu Ando for magicking this out of nowhere. The Game & Watch games were literally just random bleeps and bloops, which you can hear in the remix for sure. But he also made a whole as electronic track to go with it. And it kinda slaps. It's got this big deep bass and interesting harmony. I like it.
title: Flat Zone
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 14: Game & Watch

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 14 - Game & Watch (Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2001)\02. Flat Zone 2.mp3
description: A different composer tackled the challenge of remixing music that doesn't exist for Brawl and he definitely understood the assignment. With the space-y ambient guitar sound, he really captured the vibe of the previous Flat Zone. I do feel like this music is a bit harsher on the ears.
title: Flat Zone 2
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 14: Game & Watch
